error.signature: LLM returns values not in the specified enum when generating structured JSON output
error.regex: (hallucinated.*enum|invalid.*enum.*value|JSON.*schema.*violation|structured.*output.*invalid|LLM\ returns\ values\ not\ in\ the\ specified\ enum\ when\ generating)

verdict.summary: Even with JSON mode or function calling, LLMs can hallucinate enum values not in the schema. The model 'understands' the schema but doesn't guarantee constraint satisfaction. Only json_schema response_format with strict:true in OpenAI actually enforces enums.

dead_ends.action: Define enum in function schema and trust the model to respect it
dead_ends.why_fails: Function calling schemas are treated as suggestions, not constraints. The model can return any string for an enum field.

dead_ends.action: Use JSON mode (response_format: json_object) and assume schema compliance
dead_ends.why_fails: JSON mode only guarantees valid JSON syntax, NOT schema compliance. Fields can be missing, wrong type, or have invalid enum values.

workarounds.action: Use strict: true with json_schema response_format (OpenAI)
Validate LLM output against schema and retry on violation
workarounds.how: for attempt in range(3): output = llm_call(); if validate(output, schema): return output  # retry loop

workarounds.action: Post-process enum fields with fuzzy matching to nearest valid value
workarounds.how: from difflib import get_close_matches; valid = get_close_matches(output_val, enum_values, n=1)
